div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in Greece (MacVeagh)
div.p: 68. Your 156, October 14, 2 p.m. As you report that your colleagues, and particularly the representatives of the protecting powers under the Treaty of London of May 7, 1832, are replying to the Foreign Minister’s note in the usual manner, the Department perceives no reason why you should not do the same.

For background information to this instruction please see Department’s telegram No. 63, April 16, 6 p.m., 1924.Telegram No. 63, April 16, 1924, 6 p.m., was from the Chargé in Greece. The Department’s instruction in reply was telegram No. 46, April 17, 1924, 5 p.m. See Foreign Relations, 1924, vol. ii, pp. 271, 272.
